pi_items.source: setup_threshold_task.sh script URL and HTTP HEAD verification; direct download and file inspection with PIL
setup_threshold_task.sh script URL and HTTP HEAD verification (HTTP 200 response)

pi_items.verified_value: 120-140 are optimal values for the downloaded portrait image. At threshold 120: 40.3% black, 59.7% white. At threshold 140: 57.7% black, 42.3% white. Both achieve 100% binary conversion, 0% gray pixels, and balanced distribution (within 15-85% range).
pi_items.source: README.md lines 62-69; verified by loading primary portrait (687x1031) and applying numerical threshold algorithm with numpy

pi_items.verified_value: Verifier requires >=90% binary pixels (black <=10 or white >=245) with <=5% gray pixels (10-245 range)
Verifier requires 15%-85% black AND 15%-85% white for balanced distribution (line 50: `is_balanced = (15 <= black_percentage <= 85) and (15 <= white_percentage <= 85)`)
Score must be >= 75 to pass (3 out of 4 criteria: binary conversion, gray elimination, balanced distribution, meaningful change)

pi_items.metadata_value: task.json should contain 'metadata' field per audit instructions
pi_items.verified_value: task.json has NO 'metadata' field. Structure contains only: id, version, env_id, description, init, hooks, success

privileged_info_summary: The task downloads a real Unsplash portrait image (687x1031 pixels) and applies GIMP's threshold tool. Recommended threshold values of 120-140 result in perfect binary conversion (100% black/white pixels, 0% gray) with balanced distribution (40/60 to 58/42 split). The verifier correctly checks for >=90% binary pixels, <=5% gray, and 15-85% balance on each color to pass. However, task.json lacks the 'metadata' field entirely, so no metadata claims exist to audit.
